name: Turkey in Chocolate and Chili Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 Cups Chicken Stock, At Room Temperature
3 Medium Tomatoes, Peel, Seed and Chop (or 1 Cup Canned Italian Plum Tomatoes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the turkey, bay leaves, and rosemary in a large stock pot. Cover with water. Bring to a boil.
2. Reduce heat to low and simmer for one hour until the turkey is almost cooked through. Set aside.
3. To make the chocolate sauce (mole sauce):
a. Place the salsa, almonds, 2 cups of chicken stock, scallions, tomatoes (or canned Italian plum tomatoes), currants, pumpkin seeds, garlic, cinnamon, cloves, coriander, anise, salt, and pepper in a blender.
b. Puree on high speed.
c. Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a heavy skillet. Pour in the puree and simmer, stirring constantly for three minutes.
d. Add 2 cups of chicken stock and melt the chocolate into the sauce. Stir until combined.
e. Cover and set aside.
4. Remove the turkey from the stock pot and pat dry with paper towels. Cut into eight pieces.
5. Heat 6 tablespoons of olive oil in a heavy 12-inch skillet over medium heat. Brown the turkey pieces, making sure to drain any excess fat.
6. Pour the chocolate sauce over the turkey pieces. Cook for 30 minutes, turning the pieces occasionally.
7. To serve, place the turkey on a heated platter and sprinkle with 2 tablespoons of sesame seeds.
